records.ety: [OE. moche, muche, miche, prob. the same as mochel, muchel, michel, mikel, fr. AS. micel, mycel; cf. Gr. me`gas, fem. mega`lh, great, and Icel. mjök, adv., much. √103. See Mickle.]

defs: Great in quantity; long in duration; as, much rain has fallen; much time.
quotes: Thou shalt carry much seed out into the field, and shalt gather but little in.
Edom came out against him with much people.

defs: A great quantity; a great deal; also, an indefinite quantity; as, you have as much as I.
quotes: He that gathered much had nothing over.
notes: ☞ Muchin this sense can be regarded as an adjective qualifying a word unexpressed, and may, therefore, be modified by as, so, too, very.

defs: A thing uncommon, wonderful, or noticeable; something considerable.
quotes: And [he] thought not much to clothe his enemies.

defs: To a great degree or extent; greatly; abundantly; far; nearly.
quotes: Thou art much mightier than we.
Excellent speech becometh not a fool, much less do lying lips a prince.
Henceforth I fly not death, nor would prolong Life much.
All left the world much as they found it.
